question_text: A person wearing stiletto heels (heel area = 1 cm²) stands on a wooden floor and exerts a downward force of 600 N. The same person then lies flat on the floor, with their body in contact with an area of 0.5 m². Calculate the pressure in each situation (in Pa) and explain why stiletto heels can damage wooden floors while lying down does not.
correct_answer: Stiletto heel pressure = 600 N ÷ 0.0001 m² = 6,000,000 Pa (or 6 × 10⁶ Pa). Lying down pressure = 600 N ÷ 0.5 m² = 1,200 Pa. The stiletto heels create 5,000 times more pressure because the force is concentrated on a tiny area, which can exceed the floor's strength and cause damage. Lying down spreads the force over a large area, creating low pressure that the floor can easily support.
explanation: This question requires converting 1 cm² to m² (0.0001 m²), then using P = F/A twice to compare. The dramatic difference in pressure shows why the same person with the same weight can either damage a floor or not—it depends entirely on the area of contact. Stiletto heels concentrate force into an extremely small area, creating enormous pressure that splinters wood, while lying down distributes weight safely.
